SELECT s.name as studname, subjID, u.name as subjname, year, sem, mark
FROM Enrolment e JOIN Student s ON e.studID = s.studID
JOIN Subject u ON e.subjID = u.subjID
WHERE year = 2024;
SELECT s.name as studname, subjID, u.name as subjname, year, sem, mark
FROM Enrolment NATURAL JOIN Student s NATURAL JOIN Subject u
WHERE year = 2024;

Page 12
How do RDBMSs execute queries?
• Find all ordered products that were
quoted for over $1000 in October:
SELECT *
FROM Orders NATURAL JOIN Order_Details
WHERE QuotedPrice > 1000
AND OrderDate
BETWEEN ‘2023-10-01’ AND ‘2023-10-31’;
944 tuples
147 in October
3973 tuples
734 > 1000
113 tuples both 734 > 1000 and in October

Page 13
Query Execution Strategy
Strategy:
1.
Go through all order tuples and find the October ones
2.
Go through all OrderDetails tuples and
1.
match with condition ( > 1000)
2.
match with order tuples (on OrderNumber)
1. Fetch 147 Orders tuples in October
2. Fetch 113 tuples both > 1000 and matching Orders

Page 14
What is an index?
A separate structure that helps look up rows in a table.
The structure maps a key (the value of one of the columns) to the physical address of the row.
Users can't see the address. They are internal to the file system.
Users choose the column to index.
Primary keys are automatically indexed.

How selective is this?

Page 18
How do I choose the column to index?
• Examine the query
• Find the columns in the predicate
• Find the foreign key columns
18
SELECT *
FROM Orders NATURAL JOIN Order_Details
WHERE QuotedPrice > 1000
AND OrderDate
BETWEEN ‘2023-10-01’ AND ‘2023-10-31’;

Page 19
Then what?
• CREATE the index
• ALTER table Order ADD INDEX OrderDateIdx (OrderDate);
• Test the index
19
EXPLAIN
SELECT *
FROM Orders NATURAL JOIN Order_Details
WHERE QuotedPrice > 1000
AND OrderDate
BETWEEN ‘2023-10-01’ AND ‘2023-10-31’;

Page 20
If it's not used?
• DROP the index
• ALTER table Order DROP INDEX OrderDateIdx;
20
Indexes slow down inserts (also
updates)

Page 21
What Index do I Create?
21
SELECT *
FROM Orders NATURAL JOIN OrderDetail
WHERE QuotedPrice > 1000
AND OrderDate
BETWEEN '2023-10-01' AND '2023-10-31';
31 Matches
2023-10-01, 2023-10-02, 2023-10-03,
2023-10-04, 2023-10-05, .........
2023-10-31
... AND MONTH(OrderDate) = 'October';
ALTER TABLE Order ADD INDEX
OrderDateIdx (MONTH(OrderDate));
